Turn 2 ‚ÄĒ quadratic Magnus sweep I re-read only the three files attached in this turn. The problem is the over-G isomorphism question for őďS,őďH, with F=P‚čäG, P the free G-operator pro-2 group on x0,x1, and relators rS,rH as defined in the statement.

Turn 1 established that the kernel abelianizations agree: the relation rows arewS=(ŌÉ+3E,‚ąí2),wH=(Eg0+3E‚ąí2,ŌÉ‚ąíE),and these are equivalent over őõ=Z2 [[G]].It also left exactly the right next test: compute the quadratic Magnus obstruction after transporting by the explicit H1 -matrix.

The result of this turn is:The¬†degree-2¬†/¬†quadratic¬†obstruction¬†also¬†vanishes.

So the groups are not distinguished by H1, Fitting ideals, or the first nonabelian Magnus layer.The useful structural reduction is the same idempotent split as in Turn 1. Away from the single Ef-piece, where E=e‚ü®ŌĄ‚ü©and f=e‚ü®a‚ü©for a=ŌÉ 1‚ąíŌÄ2, the quadratic obstruction module is automatically zero: on the (1‚ąíE)-piece the target row contains the unit ŌÉ, and on the E(1‚ąíf)-piece the coefficient as‚ąí1 is a unit, with s=ŌÉ2. Thus any quadratic discrepancy there can be removed by a degree-2 IA correction.So only the Ef-piece can matter. On that piece, ŌĄ and a are averaged away, g0=s2, and the relators reduce to the pro-2 cyclic operator formsRS=(x0s)‚ąí1x0‚ąí3x12(x1,x1s),RH=x0s2x0x1‚ąí1x1s.

Use RS‚ąí1, because Turn 1 chose the row wS=(s+3,‚ąí2). The relevant H1 -matrix isU0=( s‚ąí1s‚ąí212),(s+3,‚ąí2)U0=(s2+1,s‚ąí1),matching the target row on this piece. This is the explicit fE-part of the Turn 1 row equivalence.LetAn:=X0sn,Bn:=X1sn.Write Magnus expansions as 1+L+Q+O(3), where L is linear and Q is quadratic. For RS‚ąí1,LS=A1+3A0‚ąí2B0,and a direct degree-2 expansion givesQS=3A0A0+3A0A1‚ąí6B0A0‚ąí2B0A1+3B0B0‚ąíB0B1+B1B0.

For RH,LH=A2+A0‚ąíB0+B1,QH=B0B0+A2A0‚ąíA2B0+A2B1‚ąíA0B0+A0B1‚ąíB0B1.

The U0 -transport sendsAn‚Ü¶An+1‚ąíAn+Bn,Bn‚Ü¶An+1‚ąí2An+2Bn.

This sends LS exactly to LH. LetD:=U0(QS)‚ąíQH.

The target linear relation isLn=An+2+An‚ąíBn+Bn+1=0.After reducing D modulo these linear target relations and then passing to the only possible obstruction quotient, namely setting s=1 and reducing mod 2, one obtainsD=2A2‚ąí6AB+2BA+2B2 ‚Č°0(mod2).That is exactly the required vanishing condition, because the degree-2 IA correction map has cokernelM2/(M2(s2+1)+M2 (s‚ąí1))=M2/(2M2+M2 (s‚ąí1)).So D lies in the image of the quadratic correction map. Equivalently, after the Turn 1 H1 -change of variables, there is a degree-2 correction of the generator images carryingRS‚ąí1‚Č°RH (modMagnus¬†degree¬†3)on the only dangerous idempotent piece. Since all other idempotent pieces have unit coefficient and hence no obstruction, this proves the global quadratic obstruction vanishes.A tempting shortcut would be to kill the canonical G-section and compare‚ü®x0,x1‚ą£x0‚ąí4x12‚ü©with‚ü®x0,x1‚ą£x02‚ü©.But that is not an over-G invariant: an over-G isomorphism may twist the chosen section by a 1-cocycle, exactly as Turn 1 already warned.The structural obstruction must therefore live in the Magnus tower modulo section twists, not in that raw canonical-section quotient.

ESTABLISHED: The first nonabelian Magnus obstruction vanishes. After the explicit H1 -equivalence from Turn 1, rS‚ąí1 can be matched to rH through quadratic degree, and no idempotent piece supplies a degree-2 obstruction.

GAP: This still does not prove őďS‚ČÖGőďH. The remaining issue is whether the obstruction continues to vanish through the full pro-2 Magnus tower, including possible cocycle twists of the G-section.

NEXT: Run the same obstruction machine at the next nontrivial layer. Structurally, all future obstructions again collapse to the residual Ef-piece modulo (2,s‚ąí1); the next decisive test is therefore the mod-2, s=1 Magnus tail, with section-twists included rather than the non-invariant canonical-section quotient.[QUADRATIC-CLEARED]
